- [ ] langchain-perplexity
- [ ] langchain-prompty
- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Example Code (Python)

```python
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
from langchain_core.tools import tool


@tool
def get_weather(location: str) -> str:
    """Get the weather for a location."""
    return f"The weather in {location} is sunny."


"""Reproduce the bug with minimal example."""

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o", temperature=0, use_responses_api=True)
llm_with_tools = llm.bind_tools([get_weather])

messages = [HumanMessage(content="What's the weather in San Francisco?")]

print("Step 1: Invoke LLM with responses API + tool binding")
response = llm_with_tools.invoke(messages)
print(f"  Response has tool_calls: {bool(response.tool_calls)}")
print(f"  Additional kwargs: {response.additional_kwargs}\n")

messages.append(response)

print("Step 2: Call get_num_tokens_from_messages()")
try:
    num_tokens = llm.get_num_tokens_from_messages(messages)
    print(f"  ✅ Success! Tokens: {num_tokens}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"  ❌ Error: {type(e).__name__}: {str(e)}")
    print(f"\n  Root cause: Response contains 'function_call' but")
    print(f"  get_num_tokens_from_messages() only handles 'function' field")
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
ValueError('Unrecognized content block type\n\n{\'type\': \'function_call\', \'name\': \'search_records\', \'arguments\': \'{"recordSearchDto":{"recordTypeId":"Rtxxx","filters":[],"groupingFieldIds":[],"sorting":[],"offset":0,"limit":10,"legacyModeEnabled":false,"rowOrderViewId":null,"recordTypeAlias":"Campaigns","scopeWorkspaceIds":["Wsxxxx"]},"recordTypeAlias":"Campaigns"}\', \'call_id\': \'call_xxxx\', \'id\': \'fc_xxxx\', \'index\': 0}')
```

### Description

I am trying to use `from langchain_core.messages import trim_messages` function to remove old messages from conversation history.
When providing token_counter=ChatOpenAI encounters error if the messages are run through OpenAI `responses` api contains `function_call` 
A deeper analysis showed that the trim_messages calls llm.get_num_tokens_from_messages function, which again was the source of error.
As seen here https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/blame/master/libs/partners/openai/langchain_openai/chat_models/base.py#L1790
the `get_num_tokens_from_messages` address `function` type content, but OpenAI returns `function_call` type content as declared in it's API https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/object#responses-object-output-function_tool_call-type

### System Info

System Information
------------------
> OS:  Darwin
> OS Version:  Darwin Kernel Version 24.5.0: Tue Apr 22 19:54:49 PDT 2025; root:xnu-11417.121.6~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000
> Python Version:  3.13.5 (v3.13.5:6cb20a219a8, Jun 11 2025, 12:23:45) [Clang 16.0.0 (clang-1600.0.26.6)]

-------------------

> langchain_text_splitters: 1.1.0
> langgraph_sdk: 0.3.0

> langserve

Other Dependencies

### Checked other resources

- [x] I added a clear and descriptive title that summarizes the feature request.
- [x] I used the GitHub search to find a similar feature request and didn't find it.
- [x] I checked the LangChain documentation and API reference to see if this feature already exists.
- [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package.

### Package (Required)

- [x] langchain
- [ ] langchain-openai
- [ ] langchain-anthropic
- [ ] langchain-classic
- [x] langchain-core
- [ ] langchain-cli

- [ ] langchain-perplexity
- [ ] langchain-prompty
- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Feature Description

When a tool is defined to have a Pydantic-based args_schema with default values, LangChain should ensure those defaults are passed to positional or kwargs of the tool.

## Currently

```python
from langchain.tools import tool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


class SearchArgs(BaseModel):
    query: str = Field(..., description="The query string to use for lexical search.")
    page: int = Field(1, description="The page number of results to return.")
    size: int = Field(10, description="The number of results to return per page.")


@tool("search", args_schema=SearchArgs)
def search_tool(query: str, page: int, size: int) -> str:
    """Perform a lexical search."""
    # Implementation of the search logic goes here
    return f"Search results for query: {query}, page: {page}, size: {size}"


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # This will throw: TypeError: search_tool() missing 2 required positional arguments: 'page' and 'size'
    search_tool.invoke(input={"query": "example"})
```

While `search_tool` could set defaults (e.g. `def search_tool(query: str, page: int=1, size: int=10)`), this repeats what is already defined in `SearchArgs` and violates DRY.

### Use Case

Beyond ensuring you don't need to repeat defaults, this will allow users to use additional functionality in Pydantic such as:

`Field(default_factory=...)` for dynamic defaults (e.g., default_factory=datetime.now)
@field_validator and `@model_validator` for input validation/transformation before the tool function is called
alias for field name mapping


### Proposed Solution

LangChain could easily handle this by effectively doing:

```python
input = args_schema.model_validate(args_dict_from_llm).model_dump()  # this will be a dict with defaults
tool.invoke(input=input)
```

We may want to allow folks to customize the call to `model_dump` as kwargs like `by_alias` may be important to control for some applications.

If there's consensus that this makes sense, happy to take a crack at implementation!

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
langchain-ai/langchain#34384.title: Populate default args when provided in a tool's args_schema

langchain-ai/langchain#34386.body:
### Checked other resources

- [x] I added a clear and descriptive title that summarizes the feature request.
- [x] I used the GitHub search to find a similar feature request and didn't find it.
- [x] I checked the LangChain documentation and API reference to see if this feature already exists.
- [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package.

### Package (Required)

- [x] langchain
- [ ] langchain-openai
- [ ] langchain-anthropic
- [x] langchain-classic
- [ ] langchain-core
- [ ] langchain-cli
- [ ] langchain-model-profiles
- [ ] langchain-tests
- [ ] langchain-text-splitters
- [ ] langchain-chroma
- [ ] langchain-deepseek
- [ ] langchain-exa
- [ ] langchain-fireworks
- [ ] langchain-groq
- [ ] langchain-huggingface
- [ ] langchain-mistralai
- [ ] langchain-nomic
- [ ] langchain-ollama
- [ ] langchain-perplexity
- [ ] langchain-prompty
- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Feature Description

Google Gen AI is not supported as a provider in `init_embeddings`.

Only the Google VertexAI `VertexAIEmbedding` is used within the factory function for the embeddings. The `_init_chat_model` already has Google Gen AI has a supported provider.

(In this release note)[https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-google/discussions/1422], it is mentioned that the `google_gen_ai` is the new provider that langchain recommended when using Google services.

### Use Case

I am using Google as my service provider and I used the API key as my certification method and I was refused to use `google_genai` for my embedding while my chat models use Google Generative ai. 

From the documentation, I understand that the Google Generative AI will be the new recommended package for Google services, so I just want to make sure that the migration works for embeddings as well.

### Proposed Solution

This could be done by adding:
```Python
    "google_genai": "langchain_google_genai",
```
in the mapping (dictionnary) of `_SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS` in `langchain/embeddings/base.py`.

```Python
if provider == "google_genai":
        from langchain_google_genai import GoogleGenerativeAIEmbeddings
        
        return GoogleGenerativeAIEmbeddings(model=model_name, **kwargs)
```
in the `init_embeddings` function in `langchain/embeddings/base.py`.

### Alternatives Considered

I could use Google VertexAI, but the documentation recommend Google Generative AI.

### Additional Context

_No response_

### Example Code (Python)

```python
import logging
import http.client
from typing import Literal
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END, MessagesState

# 1. Enable low-level HTTP debugging to see the raw URL and Headers
http.client.HTTPConnection.debuglevel = 1
logging.basicConfig()
logging.getLogger().setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
requests_log = logging.getLogger("urllib3")
requests_log.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
requests_log.propagate = True

# 2. Define dummy node functions
def llm_call(state: MessagesState):
    return {"messages": []}

def tool_node(state: MessagesState):
    return {"messages": []}

def should_continue(state: MessagesState) -> Literal["tool_node", "__end__"]:
    return "__end__"

# 3. Build the minimal agent
builder = StateGraph(MessagesState)
builder.add_node("llm_call", llm_call)
builder.add_node("tool_node", tool_node)
builder.add_edge(START, "llm_call")
builder.add_conditional_edges(
    "llm_call",
    should_continue,
    {"tool_node": "tool_node", "__end__": END}
)
builder.add_edge("tool_node", "llm_call")
agent = builder.compile()

# 4. Trigger the bug
# This will print the raw 'GET' request details to the console before crashing
print("\n--- STARTING REQUEST ---\n")
agent.get_graph(xray=True).draw_mermaid_png()
```

### Description

I tried to run the example from LangGraph quick start ([link](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/quickstart#full-code-example)).
I got a mermaid ink API 400 error.

I added a HTTP request debug output for the minimum reproducing example.

Maybe the `&bgColor=!white` part in the URL parameter is the problem as the `!` character is a special character.
The following part is where `!` character is added:

https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/blob/3dcafac79bb696220f8e70f8ca82fcdc4b0b0dd7/libs/core/langchain_core/runnables/graph_mermaid.py#L431-L435

### Package (Required)

### Example Code (Python)

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from langchain.agents import create_agent
from langchain.chat_models import init_chat_model
from langchain_core.tools import tool


class WeatherBaseModel(BaseModel):
    """Weather response."""

    temperature: float = Field(description="The temperature in fahrenheit")
    condition: str = Field(description="Weather condition")


model = init_chat_model(
    model="gemini-3-flash-preview",
    model_provider="google_genai"
)


@tool
def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    """Get the weather for a city."""
    return f"The weather in {city} is sunny and 75°F."

agent = create_agent(
    model,
    system_prompt=(
        "You are a helpful weather assistant. Please call the get_weather tool "
        "once, then use the WeatherReport tool to generate the final response."
    ),
    tools=[get_weather],
    response_format=WeatherBaseModel,
)

response = agent.invoke({"messages": [
    {"role": "user", "content": "What's the weather in Boston?"}
]})
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
{'messages': [HumanMessage(content="What's the weather in Boston?", additional_kwargs={}, response_metadata={}, id='56e1980d-2c82-4c87-9cae-5b7cc21fa3b0'),
  AIMessage(content=[], additional_kwargs={'function_call': {'name': 'get_weather', 'arguments': '{"city": "Boston"}'}, '__gemini_function_call_thought_signatures__': {'2ef57987-1a7c': 'Ep0BCpoBAXLI2nzBleFy/WyG7q1KI3oy4GdqcFsvz7UjZkr0B/'}}, response_metadata={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'model_name': 'gemini-3-flash-preview', 'safety_ratings': [], 'model_provider': 'google_genai'}, id='lc_run--019b4be1-f0fd-7a80-', tool_calls=[{'name': 'get_weather', 'args': {'city': 'Boston'}, 'id': '2ef57987-1a7c-4f72', 'type': 'tool_call'}], usage_metadata={'input_tokens': 150, 'output_tokens': 43, 'total_tokens': 193, 'input_token_details': {'cache_read': 0}, 'output_token_details': {'reasoning': 27}}),
  ToolMessage(content='The weather in Boston is sunny and 75°F.', name='get_weather', id='430f0bc2-d1a5-4601', tool_call_id='2ef57987-1a7c-4f72'),
  AIMessage(content=[], additional_kwargs={'function_call': {'name': 'WeatherBaseModel', 'arguments': '{"temperature": 75, "condition": "sunny"}'}, '__gemini_function_call_thought_signatures__': {'3eb5b1cf-0d0a-4033-a02f': '[REDACTED_TOKEN]'}}, response_metadata={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'model_name': 'gemini-3-flash-preview', 'safety_ratings': [], 'model_provider': 'google_genai'}, id='lc_run--019b4be1-f477-7250-a8f9-9dad0cb0c764-0', tool_calls=[{'name': 'WeatherBaseModel', 'args': {'temperature': 75, 'condition': 'sunny'}, 'id': '3eb5b1cf-0d0a-4033-a02f', 'type': 'tool_call'}], usage_metadata={'input_tokens': 219, 'output_tokens': 1625, 'total_tokens': 1844, 'input_token_details': {'cache_read': 0}, 'output_token_details': {'reasoning': 1604}}),
  ToolMessage(content="Returning structured response: temperature=75.0 condition='sunny'", name='WeatherBaseModel', id='b4dd4592-9bdd-4e9f-a34c', tool_call_id='3eb5b1cf-0d0a-4033-a02f')],
 'structured_response': WeatherBaseModel(temperature=75.0, condition='sunny')}
```

### Description

## Description

Supplying a raw schema (e.g., a Pydantic model) to  `create_agent(..., response_format=MyModel)` uses **AutoStrategy**.

For Gemini 3 chat models (`gemini-3-flash-preview`, `gemini-3-pro-preview`), the AutoStrategy check still returns `False` whenever `tools` is non-empty. As a result, the agent is forced into **ToolStrategy**, even though Gemini 3 now supports provider-native structured output with tools ([link](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/structured-output?example=recipe#structured_outputs_with_tools)).

Manually wrapping the schema in `ProviderStrategy(MyModel)` works, but users shouldn’t need that workaround.

## Expected

AutoStrategy should recognize Gemini 3 models as supporting structured output with tools, while pre–Gemini 3 variants and aliases (`gemini-flash-latest`, `gemini-flash-lite-latest`) remain blocked until they point to a 3-series backend.

langchain-ai/langchain#34517.body:
Description written by @mdrxy:

> When the middleware formats messages for summarization, it includes internal metadata like tool_calls, usage_metadata, response_metadata, etc., which can push the total token count over the model's context limit even though the actual conversation content might fit.

### Checked other resources

- [x] This is a bug, not a usage question.
- [x] I added a clear and descriptive title that summarizes this issue.
- [x] I used the GitHub search to find a similar question and didn't find it.
- [x] I am sure that this is a bug in LangChain rather than my code.
- [x] The bug is not resolved by updating to the latest stable version of LangChain (or the specific integration package).
- [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package.
- [x] I posted a self-contained, minimal, reproducible example. A maintainer can copy it and run it AS IS.

### Package (Required)

- [x] langchain
- [ ] langchain-openai
- [ ] langchain-anthropic
- [ ] langchain-classic
- [ ] langchain-core
- [ ] langchain-cli
- [ ] langchain-model-profiles
- [ ] langchain-tests
- [ ] langchain-text-splitters
- [ ] langchain-chroma
- [ ] langchain-deepseek
- [ ] langchain-exa
- [ ] langchain-fireworks
- [ ] langchain-groq
- [ ] langchain-huggingface
- [ ] langchain-mistralai
- [ ] langchain-nomic
- [ ] langchain-ollama
- [ ] langchain-perplexity
- [ ] langchain-prompty
- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Example Code (Python)

```python
from langchain.agents.middleware import SummarizationMiddleware
from langchain.agents import create_agent

summary_middleware = SummarizationMiddleware(
    model=summary_llm,
    max_tokens_before_summary=512,
    messages_to_keep=0,
)

agent = create_agent(
        name="test_summary_agent",
        model=model,
        middleware=[
            summary_middleware,
        ],
    )

model_input = "your test input " # token length should > 1024, make sure summary_middleware will work
result = agent.invoke(input={"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": model_input}]})
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
Error code: 400 - {'error': {'message': "This model's maximum context length is 262144 tokens. However, your request has 272099 input tokens. Please reduce the length of the input messages. None", 'type': 'BadRequestError', 'param': None, 'code': 400}}

-------------------
In fact, the SummarizationMiddleware's summary input is this (contain unrelated model usage message):
<role>
Context Extraction Assistant
</role>

<primary_objective>
Your sole objective in this task is to extract the highest quality/most relevant context from the conversation history below.
</primary_objective>

<objective_information>
You're nearing the total number of input tokens you can accept, so you must extract the highest quality/most relevant pieces of information from your conversation history.
This context will then overwrite the conversation history presented below. Because of this, ensure the context you extract is only the most important information to your overall goal.
</objective_information>

<instructions>
The conversation history below will be replaced with the context you extract in this step. Because of this, you must do your very best to extract and record all of the most important context from the conversation history.
You want to ensure that you don't repeat any actions you've already completed, so the context you extract from the conversation history should be focused on the most important information to your overall goal.
</instructions>

The user will message you with the full message history you'll be extracting context from, to then replace. Carefully read over it all, and think deeply about what information is most important to your overall goal that should be saved:

With all of this in mind, please carefully read over the entire conversation history, and extract the most important and relevant context to replace it so that you can free up space in the conversation history.
Respond ONLY with the extracted context. Do not include any additional information, or text before or after the extracted context.

<messages>
Messages to summarize:
[HumanMessage(content="在网络上搜索与'死锁'相关的技术文档、故障案例和处理指南，重点关注反汇编层面的分析方法和调试手段，提取典型故障现象、定位步骤、解决方案等信息。", additional_kwargs={}, response_metadata={}, id='f73acfae-f4e4-4709-8f7c-b9f8d7647ff6'), AIMessage(content='', additional_kwargs={'refusal': None}, response_metadata={'token_usage': {'completion_tokens': 27, 'prompt_tokens': 5512, 'total_tokens': 5539, 'completion_tokens_details': None, 'prompt_tokens_details': None}, 'model_provider': 'openai', 'model_name': 'Qwen3-235B-A22B', 'system_fingerprint': None, 'id': 'chatcmpl-88b48347-4b11-48a9-8042-fc7753b6c65f', 'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'logprobs': None}, id='lc_run--019b5eb5-8bd7-7611-8076-f60398467090-0', tool_calls=[{'name': 'search_pages', 'args': {'keyword': '死锁', 'in_space': False}, 'id': 'chatcmpl-tool-99d91ac2a6b2785e', 'type': 'tool_call'}], usage_metadata={'input_tokens': 5512, 'output_tokens': 27, 'total_tokens': 5539, 'input_token_details': {}, 'output_token_details': {}}),
```

### Description

when the summary_middleware work(input messages token length > 1024), it will give the model input with DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PROMPT in "from langchain.agents.middleware import SummarizationMiddleware" like this：

<role>
Context Extraction Assistant
</role>

<primary_objective>
Your sole objective in this task is to extract the highest quality/most relevant context from the conversation history below.
</primary_objective>

<objective_information>
You're nearing the total number of input tokens you can accept, so you must extract the highest quality/most relevant pieces of information from your conversation history.
This context will then overwrite the conversation history presented below. Because of this, ensure the context you extract is only the most important information to your overall goal.
</objective_information>

<instructions>
The conversation history below will be replaced with the context you extract in this step. Because of this, you must do your very best to extract and record all of the most important context from the conversation history.
You want to ensure that you don't repeat any actions you've already completed, so the context you extract from the conversation history should be focused on the most important information to your overall goal.
</instructions>

The user will message you with the full message history you'll be extracting context from, to then replace. Carefully read over it all, and think deeply about what information is most important to your overall goal that should be saved:

With all of this in mind, please carefully read over the entire conversation history, and extract the most important and relevant context to replace it so that you can free up space in the conversation history.
Respond ONLY with the extracted context. Do not include any additional information, or text before or after the extracted context.

<messages>
{messages}
</messages>

ERROR：
When the length of the messages token is very close to the maximum context input limit, any additional and irrelevant model usage information (such as tool call or usage statistics) can cause the total token count to exceed the limit, resulting in a context length error and causing the agent execution to fail！

- [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package.
- [x] I posted a self-contained, minimal, reproducible example. A maintainer can copy it and run it AS IS.

### Package (Required)

- [ ] langchain
- [ ] langchain-openai
- [x] langchain-anthropic
- [ ] langchain-classic
- [ ] langchain-core
- [ ] langchain-cli
- [ ] langchain-model-profiles
- [ ] langchain-tests

- [ ] langchain-perplexity

- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Example Code (Python)

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from langchain.agents import create_agent
from langchain_anthropic import ChatAnthropic
from langchain_anthropic.middleware import AnthropicPromptCachingMiddleware
from langchain.tools import tool

# Define a simple tool that can be called from code_execution
class GetWeatherInput(BaseModel):
    """Input for weather lookup"""
    location: str = Field(description="City name")

@tool(
    args_schema=GetWeatherInput,
    extras={"allowed_callers": ["code_execution_20250825"]},
)
def get_weather(location: str) -> dict:
    """Get weather for a location. Returns temperature and conditions."""
    return {"location": location, "temp_f": 72, "conditions": "sunny"}

# Create ChatAnthropic with advanced-tool-use beta enabled for code_execution
model = ChatAnthropic(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",
    betas=["advanced-tool-use-2025-11-20"],  # Required for code_execution
)

# Create agent with the prompt caching middleware
# This is where the bug manifests - AnthropicPromptCachingMiddleware applies
# cache_control to all content blocks, including tool_use/tool_result blocks
# called by code_execution which Anthropic API rejects
agent = create_agent(
    model=model,
    tools=[
        {"type": "code_execution_20250825", "name": "code_execution"},
        get_weather,
    ],
    middleware=[AnthropicPromptCachingMiddleware()],
)

messages = [
    {"role": "user", "content": "Use code_execution to check the weather in NYC and SF, then summarize."},
]

# This invocation fails because when Claude uses code_execution
# to call get_weather, the response contains tool_use blocks with
# caller={"type": "code_execution_20250825", ...}
# and the middleware applies cache_control to these blocks,
# causing the API to reject the request.
response = agent.invoke({"messages": messages})
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
BadRequestError: Error code: 400 - {'type': 'error', 'error': {'type': 'invalid_request_error', 'message': "Tool use result for `toolu_013QtuxLfX1Dm5JN97F9ZkWL` called by code execution cannot have cache_control. These blocks are not rendered in Claude's context."}, 'request_id': 'req_011CWd2iutykTkfUExrXfxo7'}
```

### Description

## Problem

When using `AnthropicPromptCachingMiddleware` with Claude's programmatic tool calling feature (`code_execution`), the middleware incorrectly applies `cache_control` to `tool_use` and `tool_result` blocks that were called by code_execution. The Anthropic API rejects these requests because these blocks cannot have `cache_control`.

## Background

When Claude uses the `code_execution` server tool, it can programmatically call other tools. These tool calls have a special `caller` field in the `tool_use` block:

```json
{
  "type": "tool_use",
  "id": "toolu_013QtuxLfX1Dm5JN97F9ZkWL",
  "name": "get_weather",
  "input": {"location": "NYC"},
  "caller": {"type": "code_execution_20250825", "tool_id": "srvtoolu_..."}
}
```

According to Anthropic's API, these blocks and their corresponding `tool_result` blocks **cannot have `cache_control`** because they are not rendered in Claude's context.

## Current Behavior

The `AnthropicPromptCachingMiddleware` applies `cache_control` to the last content block in the conversation, without checking if that block is a `tool_use`/`tool_result` called by code_execution. This causes:

```
BadRequestError: Error code: 400 - "Tool use `toolu_...` called by code execution cannot have cache_control."
```

## Expected Behavior

The middleware should skip `tool_use` blocks that have a `caller` field with type containing `code_execution`, and skip `tool_result` blocks whose `tool_use_id` corresponds to such a `tool_use`.

## Suggested Fix

In `ChatAnthropic._get_request_payload` (or the middleware), when applying `cache_control`, iterate through content blocks in reverse and skip any that are related to code_execution:

1. Skip `tool_use` blocks where `block.get("caller", {}).get("type", "")` contains `"code_execution"`
2. Skip `tool_result` blocks whose `tool_use_id` matches a code_execution-called tool

Apply `cache_control` to the next eligible block instead.
langchain-ai/langchain#34542.title: AnthropicPromptCachingMiddleware applies cache_control to tool_use/tool_result blocks called by code_execution, causing API errors

    print(f"Query: {query}")
    print(f"User ID: {runtime.context.user_id}")
    return "This is an example tool."


if __name__ == "__main__":

    print(x)
```

- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Example Code (Python)

```python
from langchain_openai.chat_models.base import OpenAIRefusalError, _oai_structured_outputs_parser
from langchain.messages import AIMessage
from pydantic import BaseModel

# This is a AIMessage instance I copied from a real langchain output
# which is an stuructured output from OpenAI's Responses API
# ids are masked
# It contains a refusal content block to indicate a refusal response of structured output
# This ai message will be passed to _oai_structured_outputs_parser as input to show the bug
ai_msg = AIMessage(
    content=[
        {
            "id": "rs_fake_id",
            "summary": [],
            "type": "reasoning",
            "encrypted_content": "fake_encrypted_content",
        },
        # This block represents a refusal message
        {
            "type": "refusal",
            "refusal": "refused content in string",
            "id": "msg_fake_id",
        },
    ],
    additional_kwargs={},
    response_metadata={
        "id": "resp_fake_id",
        "created_at": 1766276465.0,
        "metadata": {},
        "model": "o3",
        "object": "response",
        "service_tier": "default",
        "status": "completed",
        "model_provider": "openai",
        "model_name": "o3",
    },
    id="resp_fake_id",
    usage_metadata={
        "input_tokens": 10260,
        "output_tokens": 775,
        "total_tokens": 11035,
        "input_token_details": {"cache_read": 0},
        "output_token_details": {"reasoning": 512},
    },
)

# schena does not matter in this issue
class MySchema(BaseModel):
    foo: int


try: 
    _oai_structured_outputs_parser(ai_msg, MySchema)
except OpenAIRefusalError: 
    print("OpenAIRefusalError was raised. This is the proper behavior.")
except ValueError as e: 
    print("This is a wrong behavior. Error details:")
    print(e)
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
This is a wrong behavior. Error details:
Structured Output response does not have a 'parsed' field nor a 'refusal' field. Received message:

content=[{'id': 'rs_fake_id', 'summary': [], 'type': 'reasoning', 'encrypted_content': 'fake_encrypted_content'}, {'type': 'refusal', 'refusal': 'refused content in string', 'id': 'msg_fake_id'}] additional_kwargs={} response_metadata={'id': 'resp_fake_id', 'created_at': 1766276465.0, 'metadata': {}, 'model': 'o3', 'object': 'response', 'service_tier': 'default', 'status': 'completed', 'model_provider': 'openai', 'model_name': 'o3'} id='resp_fake_id' usage_metadata={'input_tokens': 10260, 'output_tokens': 775, 'total_tokens': 11035, 'input_token_details': {'cache_read': 0}, 'output_token_details': {'reasoning': 512}}
```

### Description

According to OpenAI Structured Output  in Responses API, [a refusal](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/structured-outputs#refusals) will be in the response payload that langchain need to consume properly.

LangChain's AIMessage successfully parsed the refusal so it contains a content block of refusal. 
However, the bug happens when the ai message is passed to `_oai_structured_outputs_parser` where a `OpenAIRefusalError` is supposed to be raised. Instead of the expected `OpenAIRefusalError`, `ValueError` was raised instead with incorrect message:
- "Structured Output response does not have a 'parsed' field nor a 'refusal' field. Received message: ..."

This is clearly as bug since refusal block exist in AIMessage

langchain-ai/langchain#34720.body:
### Privileged issue

- [x] I am a LangChain maintainer, or was asked directly by a LangChain maintainer to create an issue here.

### Issue Content

The sync callback mixin (`LLMManagerMixin`) methods are missing the explicit `tags` parameter that exists in their async counterparts (`AsyncCallbackHandler`). This inconsistency appears to have existed since the original implementation.

## Affected methods

| Method | Sync (`LLMManagerMixin`) | Async (`AsyncCallbackHandler`) |
|--------|--------------------------|-------------------------------|
| `on_llm_new_token` | No `tags` param | Has `tags` param |
| `on_llm_end` | No `tags` param | Has `tags` param |
| `on_llm_error` | No `tags` param | Has `tags` param |

Both `CallbackManagerForLLMRun` (sync) and `AsyncCallbackManagerForLLMRun` pass `tags` when invoking handlers:

```python
# manager.py:696-706 (sync)
handle_event(
    self.handlers,
    "on_llm_new_token",
    "ignore_llm",
    token=token,
    run_id=self.run_id,
    parent_run_id=self.parent_run_id,
    tags=self.tags,  # tags is passed
    chunk=chunk,
    **kwargs,
)
```

This means:
- Sync handlers receive `tags` via `**kwargs`, but it's undocumented in the method signature
- Async handlers have `tags` explicitly documented
- Users implementing sync callback handlers may not realize `tags` is available
- IDE autocompletion and type hints don't show `tags` for sync handlers

## Proposed fix

Add `tags: list[str] | None = None` to the sync mixin methods:

```python
def on_llm_new_token(
    self,
    token: str,
    *,
    chunk: GenerationChunk | ChatGenerationChunk | None = None,
    run_id: UUID,
    parent_run_id: UUID | None = None,
    tags: list[str] | None = None,  # <-- add this
    **kwargs: Any,
) -> Any:
```

Apply the same change to `on_llm_end` and `on_llm_error` in `LLMManagerMixin`.

langchain-ai/langchain#34757.body:
### Checked other resources

- [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or usage question.
- [x] I added a clear and descriptive title that summarizes the feature request.
- [x] I used the GitHub search to find a similar feature request and didn't find it.
- [x] I checked the LangChain documentation and API reference to see if this feature already exists.
- [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package.

### Package (Required)

- [ ] langchain
- [ ] langchain-openai
- [x] langchain-anthropic
- [ ] langchain-classic
- [ ] langchain-core
- [ ] langchain-cli
- [ ] langchain-model-profiles
- [ ] langchain-tests
- [ ] langchain-text-splitters
- [ ] langchain-chroma
- [ ] langchain-deepseek
- [ ] langchain-exa
- [ ] langchain-fireworks
- [ ] langchain-groq
- [ ] langchain-huggingface
- [ ] langchain-mistralai
- [ ] langchain-nomic
- [ ] langchain-ollama
- [ ] langchain-perplexity
- [ ] langchain-prompty
- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Feature Description


Add a `ChatAnthropicBedrock` class to `langchain-anthropic` that wraps Anthropic's official `AnthropicBedrock` client, providing full feature parity with `ChatAnthropic` while using AWS credentials.

**Current gap**: `ChatBedrockConverse` (in `langchain-aws`) uses AWS Bedrock's Converse API, which exposes only a subset of Anthropic-specific features. Anthropic created `AnthropicBedrock` specifically to provide 100% API parity with their standard SDK using AWS authentication, but LangChain has no wrapper for it.

**Key missing features in ChatBedrockConverse**:
1. **`parallel_tool_calls` control** - Cannot disable parallel tool execution ([Issue #34010](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/34010))
2. **`thinking` parameter** - Extended thinking mode only via manual config (not first-class parameter)
3. **`strict` in tool definitions** - Structured output validation (tool schema enforcement) throws TypeError ([Issue #775](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-aws/issues/775))
4. **Prompt caching config** - Fixed 5-min TTL vs user-configurable (5m or 1h options) ([Issue #723](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-aws/issues/723))
5. **Streaming reliability** - Multiple bugs with tool use ([Issues #140](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-aws/issues/140), [#239](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-aws/issues/239), [#432](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-aws/issues/432), [#763](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-aws/issues/763))

| Feature | ChatAnthropic | ChatBedrockConverse |
|---------|---------------|---------------------|
| `parallel_tool_calls` | ✅ Native | ❌ Not exposed |
| `thinking` | ✅ Native | ⚠️ Manual workaround |
| `strict` | ✅ Native | ❌ TypeError |
| Cache TTL | 5m or 1h (user choice) | 5min fixed |
| Streaming + tools | ✅ Reliable | ⚠️ Multiple bugs |

### Use Case


**Project Example**: I'm building a conversational AI agent that needs to execute parallel searches across multiple data sources for low-latency responses.

ChatBedrockConverse doesn't expose `parallel_tool_calls` control:

```python
# Current limitation
from langchain_aws import ChatBedrockConverse

chat_model = ChatBedrockConverse(model=model_id, temperature=0)

# ❌ CANNOT control parallel execution behavior
# - No way to explicitly enable parallel tool calls for speed
# - System prompts cannot reliably force parallel execution

llm_with_tools = chat_model.bind_tools(tools)
# Even with system prompt instructions, the model doesn't consistently
# execute tools in parallel, leading to sequential calls and high latency
```

**Real-world impact**:
- **Performance and Control**: Cannot reliably enable parallel searches → high latency, sequential execution by default
- **No workaround**: System prompt instructions don't solve this - the API-level control and manual graph design is required

**What we need**:
```python
# With ChatAnthropicBedrock - explicit control
from langchain_anthropic import ChatAnthropicBedrock

llm = ChatAnthropicBedrock(model="...", aws_region="us-east-1")

# For parallel execution (faster multi-source search)
llm_parallel = llm.bind_tools(tools, parallel_tool_calls=True)

# For sequential execution (when order matters)
llm_sequential = llm.bind_tools(tools, parallel_tool_calls=False)
```

**Broader impact**:
- Agent frameworks need explicit control over tool execution patterns
- RAG systems need parallel retrieval for performance
- Complex workflows need sequential execution for correctness
- Extended thinking mode requires first-class parameter support (not `additional_model_request_fields` hacks)
- Structured outputs need `strict` validation for production reliability

**Community evidence**: 7+ GitHub issues in the previous section document these gaps.


### Proposed Solution


Add `ChatAnthropicBedrock` to `langchain-anthropic` (not `langchain-aws`) for feature parity with `ChatAnthropic`:

```python
from typing import Any, Optional
from pydantic import Field, SecretStr, model_validator, ConfigDict
from langchain_core.language_models.chat_models import BaseChatModel
from langchain_core.utils import secret_from_env
from anthropic import AnthropicBedrock
from typing_extensions import Self

class ChatAnthropicBedrock(BaseChatModel):
    """Anthropic Claude via AWS Bedrock with full SDK feature parity.

    Uses AnthropicBedrock client to provide all ChatAnthropic features
    while using AWS credentials.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)

    # Model and inference parameters (from ChatAnthropic)
    model: str = Field(alias="model_name")
    temperature: Optional[float] = None
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, alias="max_tokens_to_sample")
    thinking: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None  # Extended thinking: {"type": "enabled", "budget_tokens": 1024}

    # AWS credentials (from ChatBedrockConverse pattern)
    aws_region: str = Field(default="us-east-1", alias="region_name")
    aws_access_key_id: Optional[SecretStr] = Field(
        default_factory=secret_from_env("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID", default=None)
    )
    aws_secret_access_key: Optional[SecretStr] = Field(
        default_factory=secret_from_env("AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY", default=None)
    )
    aws_session_token: Optional[SecretStr] = Field(
        default_factory=secret_from_env("AWS_SESSION_TOKEN", default=None)
    )

    # Client instance (excluded from serialization)
    client: Any = Field(default=None, exclude=True)
    async_client: Any = Field(default=None, exclude=True)

    @property
    def _llm_type(self) -> str:
        """Return LLM type."""
        return "anthropic-bedrock"

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_environment(self) -> Self:
        """Initialize AnthropicBedrock client after field validation."""
        if self.client is None:
            self.client = AnthropicBedrock(
                aws_access_key=self.aws_access_key_id.get_secret_value() if self.aws_access_key_id else None,
                aws_secret_key=self.aws_secret_access_key.get_secret_value() if self.aws_secret_access_key else None,
                aws_session_token=self.aws_session_token.get_secret_value() if self.aws_session_token else None,
                aws_region=self.aws_region,
            )
        return self

    # Implementation would reuse ChatAnthropic methods:
    # _generate(), _agenerate(), _stream(), _astream()
    # bind_tools() with parallel_tool_calls support
```

**Implementation strategy**:
- 80%+ code reuse from existing `ChatAnthropic`
- AWS credential handling + full Anthropic features
- Drop-in replacement for users needing feature parity

**Why `langchain-anthropic`**:
- Maintains consistency with `ChatAnthropic` (same package)
- Easier to keep synchronized when Anthropic adds features
- Natural home for all Anthropic SDK wrappers

### Alternatives Considered


1. **Improve ChatBedrockConverse** - Not feasible. AWS Converse API doesn't expose these parameters at the API level (e.g. no `disable_parallel_tool_use` support).

2. **System prompt workarounds** - Currently using this for parallel tool control. Unreliable and doesn't address other gaps.

3. **Use ChatAnthropic with Anthropic API** - Requires Anthropic API keys instead of AWS credentials. Not viable for AWS-only environments.

4. **Use AnthropicBedrock directly** - Loses LangChain integration (no LCEL, no callbacks, no LangGraph compatibility).

5. **Manual parameter passing via `additional_model_request_fields`** - Fragile, not type-safe, not validated. Poor developer experience.


### Additional Context


**Anthropic's rationale** ([from official docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/claude-on-amazon-bedrock)):
> The `AnthropicBedrock` client provides the same API surface as the standard Anthropic client, but uses AWS credentials for authentication. This ensures all new Anthropic features are immediately available on Bedrock with identical interfaces.

**Key architectural difference**:
- **AWS Converse API** (ChatBedrockConverse): Unified interface across providers → lowest common denominator
- **Anthropic Messages API** (AnthropicBedrock): Direct access → full feature parity

**Migration example**:
```python
# Before (limited features)
from langchain_aws import ChatBedrockConverse
llm = ChatBedrockConverse(model="...", region_name="us-east-1")

# After (full features)
from langchain_anthropic import ChatAnthropicBedrock

# Example 1: Sequential tool execution
llm = ChatAnthropicBedrock(model="...", aws_region="us-east-1").bind_tools(
    tools,
    parallel_tool_calls=False  # ✅ Now available!
)

# Example 2: Extended thinking mode (with tool_choice: auto only)
llm = ChatAnthropicBedrock(
    model="...",
    aws_region="us-east-1",
    thinking={"type": "enabled", "budget_tokens": 5000}  # ✅ First-class parameter!
)

# Example 3: Structured outputs with strict validation
tools_with_strict = [{
    "name": "get_weather",
    "input_schema": {...},
    "strict": True  # ✅ Enforces exact schema match
}]
llm = ChatAnthropicBedrock(model="...", aws_region="us-east-1").bind_tools(tools_with_strict)
```

**Contribution offer**: I'm happy to implement this feature following the ChatAnthropic template.
langchain-ai/langchain#34757.title: Add ChatAnthropicBedrock wrapper for full Anthropic SDK feature parity on AWS Bedrock

- [x] The bug is not resolved by updating to the latest stable version of LangChain (or the specific integration package).
- [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package.
- [x] I posted a self-contained, minimal, reproducible example. A maintainer can copy it and run it AS IS.

### Package (Required)

- [x] langchain
- [x] langchain-openai
- [ ] langchain-anthropic
- [ ] langchain-classic

- [ ] langchain-cli

- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [x] Other / not sure / general

### Reproduction Steps / Example Code (Python)

```python
"""Minimal reproduction of UnboundLocalError in langchain when clearing conversation.

  Bug: Using `aupdate_state` with `RemoveMessage(id=REMOVE_ALL_MESSAGES)` causes
  UnboundLocalError in `_fetch_last_ai_and_tool_messages` when no AIMessage exists
  in the resulting state.

  Error:
      UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'last_ai_index' where it is
      not associated with a value

  Setup:
      pip install langchain langgraph langchain-openai
      export OPENAI_API_KEY="your-key"
      python langchain_bug_repro.py

  Versions tested:
      langchain==1.2.6
      langgraph==1.0.6
  """

  import asyncio
  import os

  from langchain.agents import create_agent
  from langchain.messages import RemoveMessage
  from langchain.tools import tool
  from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
  from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import InMemorySaver
  from langgraph.graph.message import REMOVE_ALL_MESSAGES


  @tool
  def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
      """Get weather for a city."""
      return f"Weather in {city}: Sunny, 22°C"


  async def main() -> None:
      if not os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"):
          print("Please set OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable")
          return

      memory = InMemorySaver()
      graph = create_agent(
          model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
          tools=[get_weather],
          system_prompt="You are a helpful assistant.",
          checkpointer=memory,
      )

      config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "test_thread"}}

      # Step 1: Create conversation state
      await graph.ainvoke(
          {"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}]},
          config=config,
      )

      # Step 2: Try to clear conversation - THIS CAUSES THE BUG
      await graph.aupdate_state(
          config,
          {
              "messages": [
                  RemoveMessage(id=REMOVE_ALL_MESSAGES), # with just this it is already an error
                  # HumanMessage(content="Fresh start"),  # No AIMessage after removal!
              ]
          },
      )


  if __name__ == "__main__":
      asyncio.run(main())
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langgraph/pregel/main.py", line 2333, in aupdate_state
app-1       |     return await self.abulk_update_state(
app-1       |            ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langgraph/pregel/main.py", line 2306, in abulk_update_state
app-1       |     current_config = await aperform_superstep(current_config, superstep)
app-1       |                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langgraph/pregel/main.py", line 2239, in aperform_superstep
app-1       |     await run.ainvoke(
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langchain_core/runnables/base.py", line 3191, in ainvoke
app-1       |     input_ = await coro_with_context(part(), context, create_task=True)
app-1       |              ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langgraph/_internal/_runnable.py", line 473, in ainvoke
app-1       |     ret = await self.afunc(*args, **kwargs)
app-1       |           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langgraph/graph/_branch.py", line 189, in _aroute
app-1       |     result = await self.path.ainvoke(value, config)
app-1       |              ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langgraph/_internal/_runnable.py", line 409, in ainvoke
app-1       |     return self.invoke(input, config)
app-1       |            ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langgraph/_internal/_runnable.py", line 400, in invoke
app-1       |     ret = self.func(*args, **kwargs)
app-1       |           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langchain/agents/factory.py", line 1548, in model_to_tools
app-1       |     last_ai_message, tool_messages = _fetch_last_ai_and_tool_messages(state["messages"])
app-1       |                                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       |   File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langchain/agents/factory.py", line 1527, in _fetch_last_ai_and_tool_messages
app-1       |     tool_messages = [m for m in messages[last_ai_index + 1 :] if isinstance(m, ToolMessage)]
app-1       |                                          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^
app-1       | UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'last_ai_index' where it is not associated with a value
```

### Description

I am using postgres saver instead of inmemory saver. I'm trying to update the local state by removing all messages in a thread. I don't use `adelete_thread` as it would delete also history in the DB, however, I'd like to preserve it there. Thus, I am using `aupdate_state`. However, the system prompt (not sure it is of AIMessage type though) is never in the state's message list. It is prepended dynamically in `create_agent. Thus, calling with just RemoveMessage will fire an error

```python
            await self.graph.aupdate_state(
                config,
                {
                    "messages": [
                        RemoveMessage(id=REMOVE_ALL_MESSAGES),
                        # AIMessage("The local history was cleared"),
                    ]
                },
            )
```

The source code in the langchain is buggy:

```python
def _fetch_last_ai_and_tool_messages(
    messages: list[AnyMessage],
) -> tuple[AIMessage, list[ToolMessage]]:
    last_ai_index: int
    last_ai_message: AIMessage

    for i in range(len(messages) - 1, -1, -1):
        if isinstance(messages[i], AIMessage):
            last_ai_index = i
            last_ai_message = cast("AIMessage", messages[i])
            break

    tool_messages = [m for m in messages[last_ai_index + 1 :] if isinstance(m, ToolMessage)]
    return last_ai_message, tool_messages
```

It does not handle the logic where there is no AIMessage in the list of messages. Even if there is no such situation (ideally), it is still better to add safe guards to not get in the state with unbound local variable

The workaround currently is to add dummy AIMessage

### System Info

```
>>> from langchain_core import sys_info
>>> sys_info.print_sys_info()

System Information

```python
import os

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.agents import create_agent
from langchain.agents.structured_output import AutoStrategy


model = ChatOpenAI(
    model="google/gemini-2.5-pro",
    base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
    api_key=os.environ["OPENROUTER_API_KEY"],
)


schema = {
    "type": "object",
    "required": ["result"],
    "properties": {
        "result": {
            "type": "string",
        }
    },
}

agent = create_agent(model, response_format=AutoStrategy(schema))

result = agent.invoke({"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "hi"}]})
print(result)
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
ValueError: ToolStrategy specifies tool 'response_format_5549' which wasn't declared in the original response format when creating the agent.
During task with name 'model' and id '3453861c-6038-975b-db82-704ccb42d096'
```

### Description

The buggy code is here:

https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/blob/a6e8c8387882005081716821e0b55e53ed390cbf/libs/langchain_v1/langchain/agents/factory.py#L1031-L1079

When using `AutoStrategy`, the `effective_response_format` is a newly created `ToolStrategy` object with a new tool name, but `structured_output_tools` are declared upfront.

### Package (Required)

- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Reproduction Steps / Example Code (Python)

```python
"""
  Minimal reproduction showing tool call concatenation bug in merge_dicts()
  """
  from langchain_core.tools import tool
  from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
  from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent

  # Define simple tools
  @tool
  def read_file(path: str) -> str:
      """Read a file."""
      return f"Contents of {path}"

  @tool
  def search_text(pattern: str) -> str:
      """Search for pattern."""
      return f"Found matches for {pattern}"

  # Create agent that will make parallel tool calls
  llm = ChatOpenAI(
      base_url="https://your-bedrock-endpoint/v1",
      model="anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0",
      streaming=True
  )
  agent = create_react_agent(llm, [read_file, search_text])

  # Ask question that triggers multiple tool calls
  result = agent.invoke({
      "messages": [("user", "Read config.yaml and search for 'model'")]
  })

  # BUG: Tool calls get concatenated into one during streaming:
  # Expected: Two separate tool_calls: [{"name": "read_file", ...}, {"name": "search_text", ...}]
  # Actual: One merged tool_call: {"name": "read_filesearch_text", ...}
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
Error Message and Stack Trace

  GraphRecursionError: Recursion limit of 100 reached without hitting a stop condition.

  This occurs because:
  1. Agent intends to make 2 tool calls
  2. merge_dicts() concatenates them: "read_file" + "search_text" = "read_filesearch_text"
  3. ToolNode only executes 1 tool (can't find "read_filesearch_text")
  4. Agent retries indefinitely → hits recursion limit

  Additionally, with Bedrock:
  ValidationException: Value at 'messages.N.member.content.M.member.toolUse.name'
  failed to satisfy constraint: Member must have length less than or equal to 64

  This occurs when concatenated tool names exceed 64 characters.
```

### Description

 ---
  Description

  ## Problem

  The `merge_dicts()` function in `langchain_core/utils/_merge.py` concatenates tool call fields (name, id, type, function) when merging streaming chunks. This causes multiple parallel tool calls to be incorrectly merged into a single tool call.

  ## Current Behavior

  When streaming tool calls from providers like Claude/Bedrock:

  **What the LLM sends:**
  ```python
  [
      {"name": "read_file", "id": "tooluse_ABC", "args": {"path": "config.yaml"}},
      {"name": "search_text", "id": "tooluse_DEF", "args": {"pattern": "model"}}
  ]

  What merge_dicts() produces:
  [
      {
          "name": "read_filesearch_text",  # CONCATENATED
          "id": "tooluse_ABCtooluse_DEF",  # CONCATENATED
          "args": {"path": "config.yaml"}  # Only first args preserved
      }
  ]

  Expected Behavior

  Tool calls should remain as separate items in the list, NOT concatenated.

  Root Cause

  In langchain_core/utils/_merge.py, lines 59-70:

  # Current code (BUGGY)
  if (right_k == "index" and merged[right_k].startswith("lc_")) or (
      right_k in {"id", "output_version", "model_provider"}
      and merged[right_k] == right_v
  ):
      continue
  merged[right_k] += right_v  # <-- Concatenates tool call fields!

  The guard conditions don't include tool call fields (name, id, type, function), so they get concatenated on line 69.

  Impact

  1. Validation errors: Concatenated names exceed provider limits (e.g., Bedrock's 64-char limit)
  2. Incorrect execution: Only 1 tool executes instead of N
  3. Recursion errors: Agent retries infinitely because expected tools don't execute

  Proposed Fix

  Add tool call fields to the skip-concatenation guard:

  if (right_k == "index" and merged[right_k].startswith("lc_")) or (
      right_k in {"id", "output_version", "model_provider"}
      and merged[right_k] == right_v
  ) or (
      # Don't concatenate tool call fields - they should remain separate
      right_k in {"name", "id", "type", "function"}
  ):
      continue
  merged[right_k] += right_v

  This ensures tool call fields are never concatenated, preserving separate tool calls as intended.

### System Info

### Checked other resources

- [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or usage question.
- [x] I added a clear and descriptive title that summarizes the feature request.
- [x] I used the GitHub search to find a similar feature request and didn't find it.
- [x] I checked the LangChain documentation and API reference to see if this feature already exists.
- [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package.

### Package (Required)

- [ ] langchain
- [ ] langchain-openai
- [ ] langchain-anthropic
- [ ] langchain-classic
- [x] langchain-core
- [ ] langchain-model-profiles
- [ ] langchain-tests
- [ ] langchain-text-splitters
- [ ] langchain-chroma
- [ ] langchain-deepseek
- [ ] langchain-exa
- [ ] langchain-fireworks
- [ ] langchain-groq
- [ ] langchain-huggingface
- [ ] langchain-mistralai
- [ ] langchain-nomic
- [ ] langchain-ollama
- [ ] langchain-perplexity
- [ ] langchain-prompty
- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Feature Description

I would like LangChain to support approximate token counting for image and multimodal content blocks in the 
count_tokens_approximately function.

Currently, when count_tokens_approximately encounters a message with a list of content blocks (standard for multimodal models), it falls back to len(repr(message.content)). For messages containing base64-encoded images, this results in counting tens of thousands of characters as if they were text tokens, leading to massive overestimation of the token count.

This feature would allow users to get a much more accurate (though still approximate) token count for multimodal messages without needing a model-specific tokenizer.

### Use Case

I'm trying to build an application that uses trim_messages or other context window management tools with multimodal models.

Currently, I have to work around this by either stripping images from my trimming logic or implementing a custom token counter. If the default "approximate" counter is used, a single base64-encoded image can appear to consume 25,000+ tokens simply because of its string length, causing trim_messages to aggressively discard virtually all other conversation history.

This feature would help users manage context windows for multimodal models more effectively using built-in LangChain utilities.

### Proposed Solution

The  count_tokens_approximately function in libs/core/langchain_core/messages/utils.py should be updated to:

1. Iterate through content blocks when message.content is a list. 
2. Identify image_url or image blocks (and other multimodal data blocks).
3. Apply a fixed "token penalty" for images (e.g., 85 tokens per image, aligned with OpenAI's low-res base penalty) instead of counting characters. 
4. Sum these penalties along with the character counts of standard text blocks.

### Alternatives Considered

I've tried using model-specific tokenizers, but these often add unnecessary dependency complexity for a use case where a rough "approximate" count is sufficient.

Alternative approaches considered:


1. Ignoring image blocks entirely.
2. Modifying chars_per_token specifically for image data.

These don't work well because ignoring them leads to context window overflows, and base64 string length has no correlation with actual model tokenization of visual data.


### Additional Context

I have already implemented this fix in a local fork, verified it with a new test suite handling 100k+ character base64 strings, and ensured zero regressions by running the existing 141 message utility tests. I am ready to submit the PR as soon as this is reviewed.
langchain-ai/langchain#34873.title: Support approximate token counting for image blocks in langchain-core

### Reproduction Steps / Example Code (Python)

```python
from langchain_core.language_models import ParrotFakeChatModel

# Empty messages list edge case
model = ParrotFakeChatModel()
messages = []

result = model._generate(messages, stop=["stop"])

print("Result:", result)
print("Generations:", result.generations)
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test_parrot_fake_chat_model.py", line 7, in <module>
    result = model._generate(messages, stop=["stop"])
  File ".../langchain_core/language_models/fake_chat_models.py", line 361, in _generate
    return ChatResult(generations=[ChatGeneration(message=messages[-1])])
IndexError: list index out of range
```

### Description

When calling ParrotFakeChatModel._generate with an empty messages list, the method crashes with an IndexError. Internally, _generate unconditionally accesses messages[-1] to construct a ChatGeneration, which causes an out-of-range access when the list is empty.

### Related Issues / PRs

_No response_

### Reproduction Steps / Example Code (Python)

```python
"""Test to demonstrate race condition in InvokerFactory."""
import threading
import time

# Simulate the current InvokerFactory implementation
class InvokerFactory:
    _instance = None
    _renderers: dict = {}
    _initialization_count = 0

    def __new__(cls):
        if cls._instance is None:
            time.sleep(0.01)  # Simulate processing time
            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
            cls._initialization_count += 1
        return cls._instance

def thread_worker(thread_id: int, results: list):
    factory = InvokerFactory()
    results.append(id(factory))
    print(f"Thread {thread_id}: Instance ID {id(factory)}")

# Test with 10 concurrent threads
InvokerFactory._instance = None
InvokerFactory._initialization_count = 0
results = []
threads = [threading.Thread(target=thread_worker, args=(i, results)) for i in range(10)]

for t in threads:
    t.start()
for t in threads:
    t.join()

print(f"\nInitialization count: {InvokerFactory._initialization_count}")
print(f"Unique instances: {len(set(results))}")
print(f"Expected: 1, Actual: {len(set(results))}")
```

### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable)

```shell

```

### Description

## Summary

The `InvokerFactory` class in `libs/partners/prompty/langchain_prompty/core.py` implements a singleton pattern without thread safety, causing a race condition when accessed concurrently. Multiple threads can create separate instances, violating the singleton pattern and leading to inconsistent behavior.

## Description

### Location
**File:** `libs/partners/prompty/langchain_prompty/core.py`
**Lines:** 202-220

### Vulnerable Code

```python
class InvokerFactory:
    """Factory for creating invokers."""

    _instance = None
    _renderers: dict[str, type[Invoker]] = {}
    _parsers: dict[str, type[Invoker]] = {}
    _executors: dict[str, type[Invoker]] = {}
    _processors: dict[str, type[Invoker]] = {}

    def __new__(cls) -> InvokerFactory:
        if cls._instance is None:  # ← RACE CONDITION: Not thread-safe
            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
            # Add NOOP invokers
            cls._renderers["NOOP"] = NoOpParser
            cls._parsers["NOOP"] = NoOpParser
            cls._executors["NOOP"] = NoOpParser
            cls._processors["NOOP"] = NoOpParser
        return cls._instance
```

### Problem

The code uses a classic "check-then-act" pattern without synchronization:

1. **Thread A** checks `if cls._instance is None` → True
2. **Thread B** checks `if cls._instance is None` → True (before Thread A assigns)
3. **Thread A** creates instance and assigns to `cls._instance`
4. **Thread B** creates a DIFFERENT instance and overwrites `cls._instance`

This creates multiple instances instead of a single singleton, and different threads may receive different instances.

## Impact

### Severity
**Medium-High** - Affects multi-threaded applications

### Affected Use Cases
- Multi-threaded web servers (FastAPI, Flask with threading)
- Async applications with concurrent workers
- Concurrent test suites
- Any multi-threaded usage of langchain-prompty

### Consequences
1. **Registration Inconsistencies**: Renderers/parsers registered in one instance won't be available in others
2. **KeyError exceptions**: When looking up registered components that exist in a different instance
3. **State Divergence**: Different threads have different sets of registered invokers
4. **Unpredictable Behavior**: Hard-to-debug intermittent failures
5. **Memory Waste**: Multiple instances defeat the singleton pattern's purpose

## Reproduction

### Test Code

```python
"""Test to demonstrate race condition in InvokerFactory."""
import threading
import time

# Simulate the current InvokerFactory implementation
class InvokerFactory:
    _instance = None
    _renderers: dict = {}
    _initialization_count = 0

    def __new__(cls):
        if cls._instance is None:
            time.sleep(0.01)  # Simulate processing time
            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
            cls._initialization_count += 1
        return cls._instance

def thread_worker(thread_id: int, results: list):
    factory = InvokerFactory()
    results.append(id(factory))
    print(f"Thread {thread_id}: Instance ID {id(factory)}")

# Test with 10 concurrent threads
InvokerFactory._instance = None
InvokerFactory._initialization_count = 0
results = []
threads = [threading.Thread(target=thread_worker, args=(i, results)) for i in range(10)]

for t in threads:
    t.start()
for t in threads:
    t.join()

print(f"\nInitialization count: {InvokerFactory._initialization_count}")
print(f"Unique instances: {len(set(results))}")
print(f"Expected: 1, Actual: {len(set(results))}")
```

### Expected Output (Bug)
```
Initialization count: 10
Unique instances: 10
Expected: 1, Actual: 10
```

Multiple instances are created, confirming the race condition.

## Proposed Solution

Use double-checked locking with `threading.Lock` to ensure thread safety:

```python
import threading

class InvokerFactory:
    """Factory for creating invokers."""

    _instance = None
    _lock = threading.Lock()  # ← Add lock
    _renderers: dict[str, type[Invoker]] = {}
    _parsers: dict[str, type[Invoker]] = {}
    _executors: dict[str, type[Invoker]] = {}
    _processors: dict[str, type[Invoker]] = {}

    def __new__(cls) -> InvokerFactory:
        # First check (fast path, no lock needed)
        if cls._instance is None:
            with cls._lock:  # ← Acquire lock for initialization
                # Double-check after acquiring lock
                if cls._instance is None:
                    cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
                    # Add NOOP invokers
                    cls._renderers["NOOP"] = NoOpParser
                    cls._parsers["NOOP"] = NoOpParser
                    cls._executors["NOOP"] = NoOpParser
                    cls._processors["NOOP"] = NoOpParser
        return cls._instance
```

### Why This Works

1. **Fast path**: After initialization, the first check (`if cls._instance is None`) immediately returns the instance without acquiring the lock
2. **Thread-safe initialization**: The lock ensures only one thread can initialize the singleton
3. **Double-check pattern**: The second check inside the lock prevents race conditions
4. **Minimal overhead**: Lock is only acquired during the first access

### Verification

With the fix applied, running the same test produces:

```
Initialization count: 1
Unique instances: 1
Expected: 1, Actual: 1
```

All threads now receive the same instance.

## Additional Recommendations

1. **Add thread-safety tests**: Create unit tests that verify concurrent access works correctly
2. **Document thread safety**: Update docstring to mention the class is thread-safe
3. **Review similar patterns**: Check for other singleton implementations in the codebase

## Environment

- **Python Version**: 3.10+
- **Package**: `langchain-prompty`
- **Component**: `langchain_prompty.core.InvokerFactory`

## Checklist

- [x] Verified the issue exists in the current codebase
- [x] Confirmed this is not a duplicate issue
- [x] Provided reproduction steps
- [x] Proposed a solution with code
- [x] Tested the proposed fix
- [x] Assessed impact and severity

### Description

I was checking 3.14 related changes in the repo for #34441 and found that #34980 [adds 3.14 classifier for chroma](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/pull/34980/changes#diff-4590fa9e72bece80a9ab3b6a61a789e41b56fce6e59f2710fc8a53ea081d4424R20). It seems Chroma itself doesn't declare support for Python 3.14, [the work is in progress](https://github.com/chroma-core/chroma/pull/5842), and there are [multiple issues related to 3.14](https://github.com/chroma-core/chroma/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20state%3Aopen%203.14)

I'm not sure if the change in #34980 was on purpose, but it seems it's a bit early to declare 3.14 support for langchain-chroma.

from langchain.messages import HumanMessage
from langchain.agents import create_agent
from langchain.agents.middleware import wrap_model_call, ModelRequest, ModelResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

model = YOUR_MODEL # get_llm_model()

class OldResponse(BaseModel):
    response: str = Field(description="The response to the user")
class DynamicResponse(BaseModel):
    response: str = Field(description="The response to the user")

@wrap_model_call
def dynamic_response(request: ModelRequest, handler) -> ModelResponse:
    return handler(request.override(response_format=DynamicResponse))

agent = create_agent(
    model=model,
    response_format=OldResponse,
    middleware=[dynamic_response]
)

agent.invoke({"messages": [HumanMessage(content="Hello, how are you?")]})
```

from langchain_core.utils.mustache import render

# which are not in the function signature.

# 2. Redundancy in Blob.from_path
from langchain_core.documents import Blob
import tempfile
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".txt") as f:
    # Blob.from_path had a redundant 'if guess_type' check
    blob = Blob.from_path(f.name)
    print(blob.mimetype)
```

### Description

I have implemented several minor improvements to langchain-core:

Fixed Docstring: In utils/mustache.py, the render function's docstring incorrectly included partials_path and partials_ext, which were not present in the implementation.

Optimized Logic: In documents/base.py, removed a redundant if guess_type: check in Blob.from_path.

Enhanced Testing: Added a dedicated unit test suite for the Blob class in tests/unit_tests/documents/test_blob.py to ensure core stability.

langchain-ai/langchain#35050.body: ### Checked other resources - [x] This is a bug, not a usage question. - [x] I added a clear and descriptive title that summarizes this issue. - [x] I used the GitHub search to find a similar question and didn't find it. - [x] I am sure that this is a bug in LangChain rather than my code. - [x] The bug is not resolved by updating to the latest stable version of LangChain (or the specific integration package). - [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package. - [x] I posted a self-contained, minimal, reproducible example. A maintainer can copy it and run it AS IS. ### Package (Required) - [x] langchain - [x] langchain-openai - [ ] langchain-anthropic - [ ] langchain-classic - [ ] langchain-core - [ ] langchain-model-profiles - [ ] langchain-tests - [ ] langchain-text-splitters - [ ] langchain-chroma - [ ] langchain-deepseek - [ ] langchain-exa - [ ] langchain-fireworks - [ ] langchain-groq - [ ] langchain-huggingface - [ ] langchain-mistralai - [ ] langchain-nomic - [ ] langchain-ollama - [ ] langchain-perplexity - [ ] langchain-qdrant - [ ] langchain-xai - [x] Other / not sure / general ### Related Issues / PRs _No response_ ### Reproduction Steps / Example Code (Python) ```python from langchain_openai.chat_models.azure import AzureChatOpenAI from langchain.agents import create_agent from langgraph.graph.state import CompiledStateGraph from azure.identity.aio import ( DefaultAzureCredential, get_bearer_token_provider ) async with DefaultAzureCredential() as credential: token = await get_bearer_token_provider(credential, "https://cognitiveservices.azure.com/.default") llm = AzureChatOpenAI( azure_deployment="our_deployed_model_name", azure_endpoint="our_foundry_project_endpoint", azure_ad_token=token, # type: ignore api_version="2025-01-01-preview", cache=False, ) orchestrator_agent: CompiledStateGraph = create_agent( model=llm, tools=[], system_prompt="You're a helpful assistant who answers any general questions for a user directly and concisely." ).with_config(recursion_limit=10) async for event in orchestrator_agent.astream_events( input={ "messages": [ { "type": "human", "content": "Tell me a joke." } ] }, version="v2" ): yield event ``` ### Error Message and Stack Trace (if applicable) ```shell 2026-02-06 14:38:37 - ERROR - agents.orchestrator_agent - Error during streaming: Error code: 400 - {'error': {'code': 'OperationNotSupported', 'message': 'The chatCompletion operation does not work with the specified model, gpt-5.2-codex. Please choose different model and try again. You can learn more about which models can be used with each operation here: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2197993.'}} ``` ### Description ## Packages Here are the relevant installed dependencies: ``` azure-identity 1.26.0b1 langchain 1.2.9 langchain-core 1.2.9 langchain-openai 1.1.7 langgraph 1.0.8 langgraph-checkpoint 4.0.0 langgraph-prebuilt 1.0.7 langgraph-sdk 0.3.4 langsmith 0.6.9 ``` ## The Issue The`OperationNotSupported` error is occurring any time a request is made to use our deployed gpt-5.2-codex model (from our Foundry project) within the LangGraph-wrapped LangChain agent (made via `create_react_agent`). The error is specifically triggered at the first `on_chat_model_start` streamed event, in which details of the model are noted under the event. When the error is caught, here's the listed host and path: - **Host**: '{base_project_url}.cognitiveservices.azure.com' - **Path**: '/openai/deployments/{our_model_prefix}-gpt-52-codex/chat/completions' Here's a run ID where the error was triggered if that's useful: '019c3486-0dcf-7553-bd77-07ee1820585e'. One thing I noticed during troubleshooting was that, behind the scenes within the LangChain library, the path and host established for the POST request seemed incorrect as they did not match the model endpoint of the deployed model within our Foundry project (where the cognitive services endpoint is referenced, NOT the Open AI endpoint). In case it's worth noting, I was able to hit the completion endpoint on this model manually via a curl using the connection details listed under the Foundry project's deployed model. Also, this agent works for any of our other deployed models, including other gpt-5 series models. ### System Info System Information ------------------ > OS: Darwin > OS Version: Darwin Kernel Version 24.6.0: Wed Nov 5 21:30:44 PST 2025; root:xnu-11417.140.69.705.2~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6041 > Python Version: 3.12.12 (main, Nov 19 2025, 22:30:44) [Clang 21.1.4 ] Package Information ------------------- > langchain_core: 1.2.9 > langchain: 1.2.9 > langsmith: 0.6.9 > langchain_openai: 1.1.7 > langgraph_sdk: 0.3.4 Optional packages not installed ------------------------------- > langserve Other Dependencies ------------------ > httpx: 0.28.1 > jsonpatch: 1.33 > langgraph: 1.0.8 > openai: 2.0.1 > opentelemetry-api: 1.39.1 > opentelemetry-sdk: 1.39.1 > orjson: 3.11.7 > packaging: 26.0 > pydantic: 2.11.10 > pyyaml: 6.0.3 > requests: 2.32.5 > requests-toolbelt: 1.0.0 > tenacity: 9.1.3 > tiktoken: 0.12.0 > typing-extensions: 4.15.0 > uuid-utils: 0.14.0 > xxhash: 3.6.0 > zstandard: 0.25.0

```python
from langchain_anthropic import ChatAnthropic
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, AIMessage
import os

messages = [
    HumanMessage(content="hello"),
    HumanMessage(content="say world"),
    AIMessage(content="Say :  ")  # trailing space triggers the error
]

llm = ChatAnthropic(
    model="claude-haiku-3",
    anthropic_api_key=os.getenv('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'),
    max_tokens=50,
    betas=["prompt-caching-2024-07-31"]
)

response = llm.invoke(messages)
print(response)
```
